2605.03697.title: Tailored Prompts, Targeted Protection: Vulnerability-Specific LLM Analysis for Smart Contracts
2605.03697.paper_summary: Smart contracts on blockchains are prone to diverse security vulnerabilities that can lead to significant financial losses due to their immutable nature. Existing detection approaches often lack flexibility across vulnerability types and rely heavily on manually crafted expert rules. In this paper, we present an LLM-based framework for practical smart contract vulnerability detection. We construct and release a large-scale dataset comprising 31,165 professionally annotated vulnerability instances collected from over 3,200 real-world projects across 15 major blockchain platforms. Our approach leverages precise AST-based context extraction and vulnerability-specific prompt design to instantiate customized detectors for 13 prevalent vulnerability categories. Experimental results demonstrate strong effectiveness, achieving an average positive recall of 0.92 and an average negative recall of 0.85, highlighting the potential of carefully engineered contextual prompting for scalable and high-precision smart contract security analysis.
2605.03697.paper_summary_zh: 智慧合約在區塊鏈上容易受到各種安全漏洞的影響，由於其不可變性，可能導致重大財務損失。現有的檢測方法通常在漏洞類型上缺乏靈活性，並且過度依賴手動編寫的專家規則。在本文中，我們提出了一個基於LLM的實用智慧合約漏洞檢測框架。我們構建並發布了一個大型數據集，包含31,165個專業標註的漏洞實例，這些實例來自15個主要區塊鏈平台上超過3,200個真實項目。我們的方法利用精確的AST基於上下文的提取和針對漏洞的提示設計，為13個普遍的漏洞類別實現定制的檢測器。實驗結果顯示出強大的有效性，平均正回憶率達到0.92，平均負回憶率達到0.85，突顯了精心設計的上下文提示在可擴展和高精度智慧合約安全分析中的潛力。
2605.03697.author: Xing Zhang et.al.
2605.03697.authors: Xing Zhang, Keyu Zhang, Taohong Zhu, Anbang Ruan
